Notes: TURNKEY BUILD — JLCPCB-assembled PCB with pre-built modules.
Goal: minimize soldering. Only connections are plug-in connectors.
Estimated total cost: ~$25-40 for 5 assembled boards (with JLCPCB SMT assembly)
See docs/JLCPCB_ORDERING_GUIDE.md for step-by-step ordering instructions.

Component: === JLCPCB-ASSEMBLED (on PCB — no soldering by you) ===

Notes: SOIC-28 variant (not DIP) — JLCPCB places this. Pre-flash firmware via ICSP after assembly.
Output LC resonant circuit. Verify JLCPCB stock for 150uH SMD inductor >= 500mA
LC resonant cap. CRITICAL value — do not substitute. Must be C0G/NPO type, >= 200V.
Output switching MOSFET. Low Rds(on) at 3.3V Vgs.
USB-C for 5V power input. Wired for power only (no data needed). Or use 2-pin JST if preferred.
Connects to nebulizer cup adapter via JST cable
For flashing firmware. Accessible through enclosure port.
Start/stop button. Can use SMD variant if preferred.

Component: === USER-SUPPLIED (not on PCB — plug in or add separately) ===

Notes: ~$1-2. Set output to ~12V with trim pot before connecting. Plugs into JST header on PCB.
Standard USB-C cable for 5V power from any phone charger or USB port
Pre-made JST-XH pigtail cable. Connects PCB to cup adapter. ~$1 for a 5-pack.
Print from OpenSCAD files. Or order from JLCPCB 3D printing service.
Spring-loaded contacts for the cup adapter. ~$3 for a 50-pack.
Print from OpenSCAD file. Or order from JLCPCB 3D printing service.
One-time purchase ~$35. Needed to flash firmware onto the PIC16F1713.
Min order 5 boards. Use the Gerber files from the PCB design.
Depends on component count and stock. Basic parts are cheapest.
MPLAB Snap — reusable for all future boards
Add programmer cost. Boards come in pack of 5 so you have spares.
